  • API Reference
  • Porous Layers

Porous Layers#

PorousLayer is the base class for all porous components in the electrode stack (GDL, MPL). It holds static geometry (thickness, porosity, absolute permeability, contact angle) and delegates transport calculations to two pluggable model objects:

  • PorousGasDiffusionModel — effective gas diffusivity with saturation and Knudsen corrections.

  • DarcyTransportModel — capillary pressure–saturation relationship via the J-function power law.

Components#

class marapendi.porous_layers.porous_layers.PorousLayer(thickness=0.001, gas=<factory>, temperature=300.0, pressure=100000.0, porosity=1, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio=1, pore_diameter=1000000000000.0, transport_resistance_model=<factory>, two_phase_transport_model=<factory>, non_wetting_saturation=0, thermal_conductivity=1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability=1000000.0, relative_permeability_exponent=3, contact_angle=120.0, non_wetting_phase='water', wetting_phase='gas', breakthrough_pressure=None)[source]#

Bases: object

Represents a porous layer in a fuel cell or electrolyzer, defining its static geometry and material properties.

Runtime temperature-dependent quantities (RT, breakthrough_pressure, saturation_flow_resistance) are computed by update_state_at_temperature() and stored in the corresponding LayerState.

Parameters:

  • thickness (float)

  • gas (GasState)

  • temperature (float)

  • pressure (float)

  • porosity (float)

  • effective_gas_diffusion_ratio (float)

  • pore_diameter (float)

  • transport_resistance_model (PorousGasDiffusionModel)

  • two_phase_transport_model (DarcyTransportModel)

  • non_wetting_saturation (float)

  • thermal_conductivity (float)

  • absolute_permeability (float)

  • relative_permeability_exponent (float)

  • contact_angle (float)

  • non_wetting_phase (str)

  • wetting_phase (str)

  • breakthrough_pressure (float)

thickness#

Thickness of the porous layer in meters (default is 1e-3 m).

Type:

float

gas#

Initial gas composition.

Type:

GasState

temperature#

Reference temperature used at construction time to pre-compute component-level capillary properties (K).

Type:

float

pressure#

Reference pressure (Pa).

Type:

float

porosity#

Porosity of the layer (0 < porosity < 1).

Type:

float

effective_gas_diffusion_ratio#

Ratio accounting for effective gas diffusion through the porous medium.

Type:

float

pore_diameter#

Average pore diameter in meters (default is large, so Knudsen diffusion negligible).

Type:

float

transport_resistance_model#

Model used to calculate gas transport resistance.

Type:

PorousGasDiffusionModel

two_phase_transport_model#

Model used to compute liquid water flow and capillarity.

Type:

DarcyTransportModel

non_wetting_saturation#

Fraction of the pore volume occupied by non-wetting phase (0 to 1).

Type:

float

thermal_conductivity#

Thermal conductivity in W/(m·K).

Type:

float

absolute_permeability#

Absolute permeability in m².

Type:

float

relative_permeability_exponent#

Exponent for relative permeability model.

Type:

float

contact_angle#

Contact angle for the non-wetting phase in degrees (default is 120°).

Type:

float

non_wetting_phase#

Non-wetting phase identifier (‘water’ or ‘gas’).

Type:

str

breakthrough_pressure#

Capillary entry-pressure scale σ·cos θ / √(K/ε) (Pa). When None (default), computed from geometry and temperature at construction time and recomputed at the actual operating temperature by update_state_at_temperature(). Provide an explicit value to override the geometry-based calculation.

Type:

float, optional

absolute_permeability: float = 1000000.0#
breakthrough_pressure: float = None#
calculate_saturation_flow_resistance(temperature, electrolyte=None)[source]#

Resistance to non-wetting phase flow at temperature (s·m²/mol).

Parameters:

  • temperature (float) – Temperature at which to evaluate fluid viscosity and surface tension (K).

  • electrolyte (ElectrolyteSolution, optional) – Required only when non_wetting_phase == 'gas' and the wetting phase is a liquid electrolyte (provides surface_tension).

Returns:

Saturation flow resistance in m²·s/kmol.

Return type:

float

capillary_pressure_from_saturation(saturation)[source]#

Capillary pressure from non-wetting saturation via the two-phase transport model.

contact_angle: float = 120.0#
effective_gas_diffusion_ratio: float = 1#
gas: GasState#
non_wetting_phase: str = 'water'#
non_wetting_saturation: float = 0#
pore_diameter: float = 1000000000000.0#
porosity: float = 1#
pressure: float = 100000.0#
relative_permeability_exponent: float = 3#
saturation_from_capillary_pressure(capillary_pressure)[source]#

Non-wetting saturation from capillary pressure via the two-phase transport model.

set_ionomer_wet_properties(ionomer_water_content, temperature)[source]#

Update ionomer transport properties at ionomer_water_content and temperature. Override in subclasses.

set_water_film_thickness(water_saturation)[source]#

Set the liquid water film thickness from water_saturation. Override in subclasses.

temperature: float = 300.0#
thermal_conductivity: float = 1000000000000.0#
property thermal_resistance#

Thermal resistance of the layer (m²·K/W).

thickness: float = 0.001#
transport_resistance_model: PorousGasDiffusionModel#
two_phase_transport_model: DarcyTransportModel#
update_state_at_temperature(layer_state, temperature)[source]#

Write temperature-dependent capillary quantities into layer_state.

Called by set_initial_state() for every layer at stack temperature, and by set_mea_temperature() for catalyst layers at MEA temperature. The component’s own fields are not mutated.

Parameters:

  • layer_state (LayerState) – State object to populate. Sets temperature, RT, breakthrough_pressure, and saturation_flow_resistance.

  • temperature (float) – Temperature at which to evaluate all temperature-dependent quantities (K).

Return type:

None

wetting_phase: str = 'gas'#
class marapendi.porous_layers.porous_layers.GasDiffusionLayer(thickness=0.0002, gas=<factory>, temperature=300.0, pressure=100000.0, porosity=0.6, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio=1, pore_diameter=1000000000000.0, transport_resistance_model=<factory>, two_phase_transport_model=<factory>, non_wetting_saturation=0, thermal_conductivity=0.5, absolute_permeability=1e-12, relative_permeability_exponent=3, contact_angle=120.0, non_wetting_phase='water', wetting_phase='gas', breakthrough_pressure=None)[source]#

Bases: PorousLayer

Gas diffusion layer: a PorousLayer with typical GDL defaults.

Parameters:

  • thickness (float)

  • gas (GasState)

  • temperature (float)

  • pressure (float)

  • porosity (float)

  • effective_gas_diffusion_ratio (float)

  • pore_diameter (float)

  • transport_resistance_model (PorousGasDiffusionModel)

  • two_phase_transport_model (DarcyTransportModel)

  • non_wetting_saturation (float)

  • thermal_conductivity (float)

  • absolute_permeability (float)

  • relative_permeability_exponent (float)

  • contact_angle (float)

  • non_wetting_phase (str)

  • wetting_phase (str)

  • breakthrough_pressure (float)

absolute_permeability: float = 1e-12#
contact_angle: float = 120.0#
porosity: float = 0.6#
thermal_conductivity: float = 0.5#
thickness: float = 0.0002#
class marapendi.porous_layers.porous_layers.MicroPorousLayer(thickness=3e-05, gas=<factory>, temperature=300.0, pressure=100000.0, porosity=0.4, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio=1, pore_diameter=1000000000000.0, transport_resistance_model=<factory>, two_phase_transport_model=<factory>, non_wetting_saturation=0, thermal_conductivity=0.3, absolute_permeability=1e-13, relative_permeability_exponent=3, contact_angle=130.0, non_wetting_phase='water', wetting_phase='gas', breakthrough_pressure=None)[source]#

Bases: PorousLayer

Microporous layer (MPL): a PorousLayer with typical MPL defaults.

Parameters:

  • thickness (float)

  • gas (GasState)

  • temperature (float)

  • pressure (float)

  • porosity (float)

  • effective_gas_diffusion_ratio (float)

  • pore_diameter (float)

  • transport_resistance_model (PorousGasDiffusionModel)

  • two_phase_transport_model (DarcyTransportModel)

  • non_wetting_saturation (float)

  • thermal_conductivity (float)

  • absolute_permeability (float)

  • relative_permeability_exponent (float)

  • contact_angle (float)

  • non_wetting_phase (str)

  • wetting_phase (str)

  • breakthrough_pressure (float)

absolute_permeability: float = 1e-13#
contact_angle: float = 130.0#
porosity: float = 0.4#
thermal_conductivity: float = 0.3#
thickness: float = 3e-05#

Transport models#

class marapendi.porous_layers.darcy.DarcyTransportModel(J_function_exponent=2)[source]#

Bases: object

Model for calculating non-wetting phase transport in porous layers using a Darcy-based approach.

Parameters:

J_function_exponent (float)

J_function_exponent#

Exponent in the non-wetting phase capillary pressure-saturation relation.

Type:

float

J_function_exponent: float = 2#
calculate_equivalent_flow_resistance(cell_side)[source]#

Compute flow resistance for each porous layer on cell_side and return the total.

Parameters:

cell_side (FuelCellSide) – Cell side whose porous_layers list is iterated.

Returns:

Sum of equivalent_flow_resistance across all porous layers.

Return type:

float

calculate_non_wetting_saturation(layer, layer_state, upstream_capillary_pressure=0)[source]#

Calculate the non-wetting saturation distribution across the porous layer.

Parameters:

  • layer (PorousLayer) – Porous layer being analyzed (provides static geometry and permeability).

  • layer_state (LayerState) – Runtime state for this layer; reads non_wetting_flux, saturation_flow_resistance, and breakthrough_pressure; writes upstream_saturation, downstream_saturation, non_wetting_saturation, and downstream_capillary_pressure.

  • upstream_capillary_pressure (array-like, optional) – Capillary pressure at the upstream boundary (Pa, default is 0).

  • mask (array-like of bool, optional) – Boolean mask selecting which elements to update; all elements updated when None.

capillary_pressure_from_saturation(state, non_wetting_saturation)[source]#

Compute the capillary pressure from non-wetting saturation using the inverse J-function.

Parameters:

  • state (LayerState) – Object providing breakthrough_pressure (Pa).

  • non_wetting_saturation (float) – Non-wetting saturation level (0–1).

Returns:

Capillary pressure (Pa).

Return type:

float

saturation_from_capillary_pressure(state, capillary_pressure)[source]#

Compute the non-wetting saturation from capillary pressure using the J-function relation.

Parameters:

  • state (LayerState) – Object providing breakthrough_pressure (Pa).

  • capillary_pressure (float) – Capillary pressure (Pa).

Returns:

Non-wetting saturation (0–1).

Return type:

float

class marapendi.porous_layers.diffusion.PorousGasDiffusionModel(water_saturation_exponent=3.0)[source]#

Bases: object

Porous media gas transport model with water saturation and Knudsen corrections.

Parameters:

water_saturation_exponent (float)

water_saturation_exponent#

Exponent for the empirical water saturation correction (n.d.).

Type:

float

gas_transport_resistance(layer, state, species='o2')[source]#

Total diffusion resistance for species through layer given state (s/m).

Parameters:

species (str)

Return type:

float

knudsen_diffusivity(layer, temperature, molecular_weight)[source]#

Knudsen diffusivity in the porous layer.

Parameters:

  • layer (PorousLayer) – Contains pore diameter (m).

  • temperature (float) – Temperature (K).

  • molecular_weight (float) – Molecular weight (kg/kmol).

Returns:

Knudsen diffusivity (m^2/s).

Return type:

float

molecular_diffusion_effective_length(layer, water_saturation=0)[source]#

Effective diffusion length accounting for porosity and water saturation.

Parameters:

  • layer (PorousLayer) – Layer with thickness and effective diffusion ratio.

  • water_saturation (float, optional) – Water saturation (n.d.).

Returns:

Effective diffusion length (m).

Return type:

float

molecular_diffusion_resistance(layer, diffusion_coefficient, water_saturation=0)[source]#

Molecular diffusion resistance through the layer.

Parameters:

  • layer (PorousLayer) – Layer properties.

  • diffusion_coefficient (float) – Binary diffusion coefficient (m^2/s).

  • water_saturation (float, optional) – Water saturation (n.d.).

Returns:

Diffusion resistance (s/m).

Return type:

float

total_diffusion_resistance(layer, temperature, diffusion_coefficient, molecular_weight, water_saturation)[source]#

Total diffusion resistance combining molecular and Knudsen contributions.

Parameters:

  • layer (PorousLayer) – Layer properties.

  • temperature (float) – Temperature (K).

  • diffusion_coefficient (float) – Molecular diffusion coefficient (m^2/s).

  • molecular_weight (float) – Molecular weight (kg/kmol).

  • water_saturation (float) – Water saturation (n.d.).

Returns:

Total resistance (s/m).

Return type:

float

water_saturation_correction(water_saturation)[source]#

Correction factor for effective diffusivity due to liquid water presence.

Parameters:

water_saturation (float) – Water saturation (n.d.).

Returns:

Correction factor (n.d.).

Return type:

float

water_saturation_exponent: float = 3.0#
